analysis.problem: Lack of clarity on the difference between studying and learning in educational contexts
analysis.root_cause: Insufficient emphasis on the process of acquiring new understanding and skills in formal education

proposed_actions.action: Develop and implement more interactive and immersive learning experiences
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase student engagement and retention rates by 20% within the next 2 years

proposed_actions.action: Integrate AI-powered tools to personalize learning paths and provide real-time feedback
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Improve student outcomes by 15% within the next 3 years

evidence: The article 'Studying or Learning? Did You Know That There's a Huge...' highlights the difference between studying and learning
The YouTube transcript 'How AI is Changing Learning and Development in 2025' discusses the potential of AI in transforming education
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domains of cognition, social structures, and technology, as it affects how individuals acquire knowledge and skills, and how societies approach education and personal development
analysis.summary: The lack of clarity between studying and learning hinders effective education, and addressing this issue can improve learning outcomes and student engagement. Implementing interactive learning experiences and AI-powered tools can help bridge this gap.

raw_items.title: Education
raw_items.summary: Education is the transmission of knowledge and skills and the development of character traits. Formal education happens in a complex institutional framework, like public schools. Non-formal education is also structured but occurs outside the formal schooling system, while informal education is unstructured learning through daily experiences. Formal and non-formal education are divided into levels

raw_items.title: Learning
raw_items.summary: Learning is the process of acquiring new understanding, knowledge, behavior, skills, values, attitudes, and preferences. The ability to learn is possessed by humans, other animals, and some machines. There is also evidence for some kind of learning in certain plants.
